title: CECO Environmental and Thermon Group Holdings Announce Election Deadline for Thermon Stockholders to Elect Form of Merger Consideration
author: Na
description: CECO Environmental has set May 22, 2026 as the election deadline for Thermon stockholders to choose their merger consideration form in the pending acquisition. Thermon shareholders can elect to receive 0.8110 CECO shares, a mix of 0.6840 CECO shares plus $10 cash, or $63.89 in cash. The transaction is expected to close on June 1, 2026, subject to stockholder approval and customary closing conditions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CECO is acquiring Thermon, a diversified industrial technology company, which represents significant growth through acquisition. The company is actively progressing toward closing the transaction with clear timelines and stockholder engagement processes in place.

Thermon is being acquired by CECO. While the acquisition itself is a significant corporate event, the announcement is procedural in nature, setting election deadlines for stockholders. The sentiment is neutral as this represents a transaction already agreed upon with clear terms being executed.

title: BRODSKY & SMITH SHAREHOLDER UPDATE: Notifying Investors of the Following Investigations: KalVista Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq – KALV), Thermon Group Holdings, Inc. (NYSE – THR), Sila Realty Trust, Inc. (NYSE – SILA), Lisata Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq – LSTA)
author: Brodsky & Smith
description: Law firm Brodsky & Smith has initiated investigations into four merger transactions, examining whether the boards of KalVista Pharmaceuticals, Thermon Group Holdings, Sila Realty Trust, and Lisata Therapeutics breached fiduciary duties by failing to conduct fair processes and potentially underpaying shareholders. The deals range in value from approximately $1.9 billion to $2.4 billion.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Under investigation for potential breach of fiduciary duties regarding fair process and fair value in $27.00 per share acquisition by Chiesi Group
Under investigation for potential breach of fiduciary duties regarding fair process and fair value in stock and cash transaction valued at $2.2 billion
Under investigation for potential breach of fiduciary duties regarding fair process and fair value in $30.38 per share all-cash acquisition by Blue Owl Capital affiliates
Under investigation for potential breach of fiduciary duties regarding fair process and fair value in $4.00 per share cash plus contingent value rights acquisition by Kuva Labs

title: CECO Environmental and Thermon to Host Joint CEO Fireside Chat at Upcoming Roth Conference
author: Na
description: CECO Environmental Corp. and Thermon Group Holdings, Inc. announced a joint fireside chat featuring both CEOs at the 38th Annual Roth Conference on March 23, 2026. The webcast will be available on both companies' investor relations websites. CECO also recently raised its full year 2026 orders outlook to exceed $1.5 billion, with the Thermon transaction remaining on track to close in mid-2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company raised full year 2026 orders outlook to exceed $1.5 billion, indicating strong business momentum and market demand. The upcoming Thermon acquisition remains on track for mid-2026 closure, suggesting confidence in strategic growth plans.

Thermon is being acquired by CECO with the transaction on track to close in mid-2026, representing a significant strategic transaction. The joint investor conference appearance demonstrates continued engagement with stakeholders during the acquisition process.

title: Meros Investment Management Sold 396,804 Shares of Photronics Stock
author: John Ballard
description: Meros Investment Management reduced its stake in Photronics by 396,804 shares in Q4 2025, selling approximately $10 million worth. The fund retained 108,004 shares valued at $3.4 million, representing 1.4% of AUM. The sale reflects a value investing strategy to lock in gains after Photronics' strong 74.2% annual performance, while the fund reallocated capital to other undervalued holdings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While Photronics showed strong performance (74.2% annual gain), Meros sold shares to lock in gains, suggesting the fund views the stock as fairly valued rather than undervalued. This is a profit-taking move rather than a bearish signal.
Listed as Meros' top holding at 6.6% of AUM (~$16.6 million), indicating strong conviction and continued confidence in the position.
Meros was actively buying Magnite in Q4 2025 while it was trading down, indicating the fund sees undervalued opportunity and conviction in future upside.
Meros reduced its stake in Thermon alongside Photronics after strong outperformance, suggesting a similar profit-taking strategy rather than negative outlook.
Meros was buying Amplitude in Q4 2025 while it traded down, indicating the fund believes it is undervalued with more upside potential.

title: $HAREHOLDER ALERT: The M&A Class Action Firm Announces An Investigation of Thermon Group Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: THR)
author: Prnewswire
description: Monteverde & Associates PC, a securities class action firm, has announced an investigation into Thermon Group Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: THR) regarding its proposed sale to CECO Environmental Corp. The investigation seeks to determine whether the merger terms are fair to Thermon shareholders, who may elect to receive various combinations of cash and CECO stock.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement of a class action investigation into the fairness of the company's merger deal raises concerns about potential shareholder value and deal terms, suggesting possible legal challenges ahead.

CECO is the acquiring company in the transaction. While the investigation could impact the deal, the article does not provide specific information about CECO's operations or prospects, warranting a neutral stance.
